The '''''Crynyd''''' was the name given to an [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] after its capture by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] at a [[battle in the Milagro system]].

==History==
In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] the Star Destroyer was commanded by the sadistic [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Purdy M. Trico]] and was found amidst a battle in a [[star system/Legends|star system]] when a New Republic [[gunboat/Legends|gunboat]] seriously damaged its bow. With its [[task force/Legends|task force]] consisting of about thirty support craft and [[transport/Legends|transports]], she [[hyperspace/Legends|hyperspaced]] to [[Milagro system/Legends|Milagro system]] for repairs.<ref name="DFJK" />

She arrived shortly after the main [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces withdrew from the devastated world of [[Milagro/Legends|Milagro]]. The [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|dreadnaught]] ''[[New Hope]]'', remained on-station as the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] in the area. As his ship was damaged, and the [[Dreadnaught/Legends|Dreadnaught]] had a strong hull, Trico ordered an attack with fighters, avoiding to reveal her location.<ref name="DFJK" /><ref name="NEC" />

However she was intercepted by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] and the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' piloted by [[Jan Ors]]. The two ships happened to exit hyperspace near her location, revealing it to Captain [[Tola_(Galactic_Civil_War)|Tola]], who commanded the battle from the ''New Hope''; and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] who led a group of [[squadron/Legends|squadron]]. The Destroyer's escorts had formed a protective screen around her, and its computer commanded the [[fire-control center]] of each one, maximizing their efficiency, but Skywalker, guided by [[the Force/Legends|Force]], managed to lead their ships inside.<ref name="DFJK" />

Trico shot his [[executive officer/Legends|XO]] for failing to monitor the dreadnaught's movements and, despite the crew's better judgment, ordered to break the formation in order to be able to shot at the annoying two ships, but it was then when Solo in the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' with his group attacked directly Trico's ship.<ref name="DFJK" />

For the next three [[standard hour/Legends|hours]] The Destroyer received a lot of damage, the [[hull/Legends|hull]] was breached and lost half of her [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser batteries]] but Trico insisted in his stance, threatening any insubordination with death. When all was desperate, the weapons-control officer shot him in the head, and it took only two [[standard minute/Legends|minutes]] to surrender unconditionally.<ref name="DFJK" />

===New Republic service===
The ship was renamed ''Crynyd'' after Rebel hero [[Arvel Crynyd/Legends|Arvel Crynyd]], who had lost his life when his fighter crashed into the ''[[Executor/Legends|Executor]]''{{'s}} bridge at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="NEC" /> 

After refitting, the ''Crynyd'' was assigned to [[Solo Fleet]] in the hunt for [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]. During the attempt to trap the [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']] in the [[Battle of Vahaba]], the ''Crynyd'' and the [[Skyhook (Star Destroyer)|''Skyhook'']] defended the Imperial [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor]] ''[[Stellar Web]]''. Right after Zsinj's escape from Vahaba, the ''Crynyd'' participated in the battle with Zsinj's backup force at the [[Battle of Selaggis]]. In the battle, the ''Crynyd'' assisted in the capture of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Red Gauntlet]]''.

After the New Republic alliance with the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], the ''Crynyd'' and numerous other [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] were destroyed while fighting [[High Admiral]] [[Treuten Teradoc]]'s forces in a [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|campaign after Zsinj's death]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]], the ship is stated to be a [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]].
